title: Printing a board
excerpt:
Hello Monday.com! 
I am new and have thoroughly enjoyed building my processes through Monday! 
I am in need of printing and cannot find a solution. 
Can you guide me in the printing process? 
Thanks!

posts.author: CRP (Christopher Phifer)
posts.cookedHtml:

I am new and have thoroughly enjoyed building my processes through Monday!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @CRP and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

What do you mean when you say “printing process”?

What do you want to print exactly and where from? In the UI?

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: CRP (Christopher Phifer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Matias,

Can I print any part of my worksheet?
I don’t see a print option anywhere.

Thanks.

Sent to you by: Christopher Phifer

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again @CRP,

I will move this topic to another section since this one is for technical questions regarding the apps framework (for developers).

They will reach out to you soon with an answer.

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @CRP

you can use the app DocExport PDF Generator to print out your boards.

The app uses Word templates with placeholders. So you can specify which columns you want to export and create your own design. You can also use tables inside the Word template to reproduce the look of monday. Here you can see how to get the color coding for status values and groups in your report:
https://www.docexport.com/help/template/colors/

It’s also possible to create a monday automation so that the report is automatically send via email every day / week / month.
In the DocExport Template Gallery you can find examples you can then modify to fit your needs.

DocExport works with formulas, mirror-columns, images, QR-Codes, Charts and Column Summaries.
